purpose: Rules for deciding whether a name should be modeled as a Brand in History Go, and how it should be classified.

core_definition: A brand is a name, term, design, symbol, or other feature that identifies one seller's goods or services as distinct from others.
A brand is also an intangible asset that creates distinctive images and associations in stakeholders' minds.

history_go_definition.working_definition: In History Go, a Brand is a socially recognized name with identity beyond a generic location label. It can be commercial, professional, subcultural, retail, gallery-based, hospitality-based, legacy, signage-based, or venue-based, as long as the name carries distinct recognition in public memory.
history_go_definition.important_note: A Place is not automatically a Brand, and a Brand is not automatically a Place. Places should be reserved for sites with independent historical, architectural, institutional, or urban significance. Brands may operate under, within, or around such places.
history_go_definition.place_definition_for_history_go: In History Go, a Place should normally be a site of historical significance, architectural weight, institutional importance, or strong urban/topographical meaning. Ordinary businesses, galleries, restaurants, bars, and clubs should usually remain in the brand layer unless the site itself is a major historical place.

decision_logic.minimum_rule: A candidate should satisfy at least 2 core criteria, and should not fail any hard exclusion.

core_criteria.identity_autonomy: The name functions as an identity in itself, not just as a street address or generic location label.
core_criteria.recognition: The name is recognized by a public, scene, profession, customer group, or cultural memory.
core_criteria.distinctive_association: The name carries distinctive associations such as quality, prestige, style, ideology, subculture, trust, or historical memory.
core_criteria.symbolic_or_visual_presence: The name has a logo, signage, facade identity, packaging, visual language, or strong typographic/graphic presence.
core_criteria.repeatability_beyond_one_building: The identity can travel beyond one building: through products, media, reputation, community, or multi-site presence.
core_criteria.memory_value: The name persists in collective memory even if the original business is closed.

hard_exclusions: Pure place names with no separate identity layer
Generic institutions used only as locations
Infrastructure names with no brand recognition
Ordinary chain tenants with weak symbolic value
Temporary event titles with no durable identity
Historically significant main sites that should primarily be modeled as places

include: Professional brands such as major law firms or architecture firms
Subculture stores, record shops, scene names, cult venues, clubs, bars, restaurants and cafés when the name functions as a recognized identity
Gallery names when the gallery is an actor/brand rather than a historical main place
Legacy brands and historical trade names
Signage brands and iconic advertising signs
Venue brands when the venue name itself has strong public identity and is not better modeled as a historical main place
Oslo-associated brands with strong city-memory value
exclude_by_default: Places that are primarily historical, architectural, institutional, or urban landmarks
Museums, libraries, theatres, or cultural houses modeled primarily as historical main places
Weak retail chains with little symbolic value
Generic franchise outlets unless they have exceptional local symbolic force

brand_groups.oslo_origin_brand: Founded in Oslo or clearly originating in Oslo.
brand_groups.oslo_based_brand: Strongly based in Oslo even if origin is mixed or elsewhere.
brand_groups.oslo_associated_brand: Strongly associated with Oslo in public memory, trade, or culture.
brand_groups.subculture_brand: Carries scene, cult, underground, indie, or countercultural value.
brand_groups.professional_brand: Professional-services identity with public recognition, such as law or architecture firms.
brand_groups.legacy_brand: Historically important brand that may now be closed or transformed.
brand_groups.signage_brand: Primarily known through signage, facade identity, or iconic visual mark.
brand_groups.venue_brand: A venue, restaurant, bar, club, café, gallery, or similar actor-name that functions as a brand in public consciousness but does not outrank the place layer.

brand_type_examples.fashion_brand: Apparel, streetwear, accessories, or fashion-led retail identity.
brand_type_examples.luxury_brand: High-prestige brand with strong status signaling.
brand_type_examples.coffee_brand: Coffee roaster, café identity, or coffee-led product/venue brand.
brand_type_examples.restaurant_brand: Restaurant or dining name with strong standalone identity.
brand_type_examples.music_store: Record stores, instrument stores, or music retail with scene value.
brand_type_examples.music_venue: Concert/club venue with durable name recognition, unless it is better modeled as a major historical place.
brand_type_examples.bookstore_brand: Bookstore with strong identity beyond ordinary retail.
brand_type_examples.institution_brand: Institution that truly functions as a public brand, not just a place label.
brand_type_examples.historic_company: Long-running or historically important commercial company.
brand_type_examples.media_brand: Newspaper, magazine, publisher, or media identity.
brand_type_examples.specialty_store_brand: Niche retail concept with strong recognition.
brand_type_examples.gallery_brand: Gallery or art-space name treated as a brand actor rather than a historical main site.

state_system.catalog: Approved active brand for app use.
state_system.strong: Strong candidate, likely brand, needs final place mapping or review.
state_system.borderline: Plausible candidate, but weaker or more ambiguous.
state_system.move_to_places: Should live primarily in places because the site itself is historically/architecturally/institutionally primary.

place_vs_brand_rules.core_rule: Do not decide based on whether something is physical. Decide based on whether the name should be the primary historical place object or a brand/actor operating under a place.
place_first_when: The site has independent historical significance
The site has major architectural weight
The site has institutional/public significance beyond one operator
The place would still matter historically even if today's operator disappeared
brand_first_when: The name is an actor, business, venue concept, gallery, restaurant, bar, club, or store operating under a larger place framework
The name is recognized as a distinct identity with its own public memory
The site is important mainly because of the operator-name, not because the physical place itself is a major historical site
examples_place_first: MUNCH when treated as the museum place
Major historical theatres, museums, libraries, parks, landmarks, or architectural complexes
place_vs_brand_rules.borderline_rule: If a name is both a place and a brand, keep the place object only when the place has independent historical or architectural primacy. Otherwise keep it in brands.

recommended_thresholds.move_to_places: Fails brand threshold or is fundamentally place-first

verification_policy.verified: Strong source support and clean identity/place distinction.
verification_policy.verified_legacy: Historically verified, even if inactive now.
verification_policy.manual_curated: Accepted by curator based on strong qualitative judgment.
verification_policy.verified_unmapped: Clearly a brand, but not yet mapped to a place.

mapping_policy.rule: A brand may map to multiple places. Use direct place match first; otherwise use best-fit only when the relationship is obvious and documented.
do_not_do: Do not force a brand into a by-category place if a better place exists in another category
Do not create a place mapping from vibe alone
Do not map a place to itself just because the names match
Do not move a brand to places merely because it is a venue; first test whether the place itself has historical or architectural primacy

brand_no_or_place_first: Mathallen when treated as the historical/main place
Dansens Hus when treated as the institutional place
